manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: horse, mama, job, situation, key, pain, road, attention, bar.
Your opponent's team words are: water, girl, couple, floor, evidence, club, honey, death.
Distractor words are: school, doctor, world, lord, uncle, bed, stuff.
The kill words are: window.

content.thinking:

Opponent's words: water, girl, couple, floor, evidence, club, honey, death

Distractor words: school, doctor, world, lord, uncle, bed, stuff

"BLOW" and "HAND" both have inappropriate connotations and weak links. "PAINTING" or "ART" might work with bar but doesn't extend far enough.

"RAILROAD" could connect through road as well.

Checking "BLUES" - it bridges mama (blues songs), pain (blues themes), and bar (blues bar). I should verify this doesn't overlap with the opponent's words like water, girl, couple, floor, evidence, club, honey, or death, and the distractors like school, doctor, world, lord, uncle, bed, stuff. The kill word window has no connection to blues either. This seems like a solid three-word option, possibly four if road works.

Let me explore alternatives. "FOCUS" could connect attention, job, key, and situation with reasonable strength. "TENDER" links bartender, tender attention, and tender pain, though it risks confusion with couple (tender love) from the opponent's list. "MAIN" has potential across road, key, attention, situation, and job, but the associations feel looser. "CENTRAL" only yields key and situation weakly. I should keep evaluating what gives the strongest, most reliable connections.

"PAY" stands out—it clearly connects pay attention and pay for job, with possible extensions to bar and pain. The gerund form "PAYING" is less reliable. Then there's "HORSE," which branches into numerous compounds: dark horse, workhorse, hobby horse, iron horse, steel horse, plus phrases like horse sense and horse around. "DARK" could work across horse, bar, road, and situation, though the links feel somewhat loose. I need to keep testing which word creates the most solid bridges to the target list.

"RACE" connects strongly to horse race, road race, and job (through rat race), plus the metaphorical race for attention. Checking against the opponent's words—water, girl, couple, floor, evidence, club, honey, death—none of these pair naturally with race. The distractors like school, doctor, world, and lord also don't create compelling associations, so "RACE" appears clean. I should also revisit "BLUES" since it has potential: mama blues, pain and suffering in blues music, blues bar, and the blues road all seem viable connections. "MUSIC" connects to key (musical key), bar (measure), and mama (songs feature mothers). That's solid for 2-3 words.

Weighing the strongest candidates: "BLUES" works exceptionally well across all four words—mama, pain, bar, and road each have clear blues associations. "RACE" also hits four words strongly with horse, road, job, and attention. "SPACE" is more limited at just two solid connections.

I'm going with "BLUES" since the semantic links feel most natural across the full set. horse race is incredibly strong, and road race works well too. Job connects through rat race, and attention through the competitive sense. Blues has solid connections—mama and pain are natural to blues music, blues bar is direct, though the road connection feels weaker. I'll go with RACE since the first two targets have such clear, immediate associations.
